rulings.Case Title: SAM HASTY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 7, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Brand Energy Services of California, Inc.¿s motion for renewal of motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that new or different facts, circumstances or law exist as required by C.C.P. sections 437c(f)(2) and 1008(b). The court declines to exercise its inherent authority to sua sponte reconsider its prior order. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW WARNER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 7, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant Lear Siegler Diversified Holdings Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant¿s product(s) at issue contained asbestos. Plaintiff also failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether liability may be imposed on defendant regarding exposure to asbestos from the use of defendant¿s product(s) in conjunction with raw asbestos. (See O¿Neil v. Crane Co. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 335; Barker v. Hennessy Industries, Inc., (May 22, 2012) 2012 WL 1850538.) Bettencourt v. Hennessy Industries, Inc., (May 4, 2012) 2012 WL 1570763, is distinguishable because there plaintiff alleged that the only products that could be used in conjunction with Hennessy¿s product were asbestos-containing brake linings while here, plaintiff¿s own deposition testimony demonstrates that defendant¿s product(s) at issue did not require asbestos to function nor was asbestos always used in conjunction with defendant¿s product(s) at issue. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 7, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)
